title: Pleistocene giant slope failures offshore Arauco Peninsula, Southern Central Chile
abstract: Pleistocene giant submarine slope failures offshore the Arauco Peninsula (~37°–38°S), southern central Chile, are documented using multibeam bathymetry and PARASOUND sub-bottom profiler data. Multiple mass transport deposits with volumes up to several hundred km³ are identified and dated using sediment cores. The failures are attributed to seismic triggering, excess pore pressure, and volcanic ash loading during the Pleistocene.

location.description: Offshore Arauco Peninsula (~37°–38°S) southern central Chile; Pleistocene giant submarine slope failures documented by multibeam and PARASOUND

seismic_lines.name: SONNE PARASOUND profiles — Arauco slope failures ~37°–38°S

acquisition.source_type: PARASOUND sub-bottom profiler and multibeam echosounder

_data_note: Journal of the Geological Society paper; behind paywall. SONNE cruise (likely SPOC SO161). Repository not confirmed.

processing.summary: Multibeam bathymetry and PARASOUND sub-bottom profiler data from SONNE cruise(s) offshore Arauco Peninsula (~37°–38°S) document Pleistocene giant submarine slope failures. Multiple mass transport deposits are identified and dated using sediment cores. Failure mechanisms attributed to seismic triggering, pore pressure, and volcanic ash loading.
workflow: 4. Sediment core analysis and radiocarbon/tephrochronology dating
processing.notes: JGS slope stability paper related to SPOC data; companion to Geersen et al. 2011 Tectonics forearc paper
